---
title: "Chapter 4 — Progress Timeline"
subtitle: "Ch 4 · living document of progress"
author: Cali Pfleger
date: last-modified
bibliography: ../literature/bibliography.bib
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: true
    preview-links: auto
    footer: "Ch 4 progress · auto-updated nightly · calipfleger@ucsb.edu"
    fig-align: center
    self-contained: false
---

## How this deck works {.smaller}

This is a **living timeline** of progress on Chapter 1.

- The nightly pipeline appends a date-stamped section after midnight every day.
- Each daily section includes a short summary of what was worked on plus any new figures.
- Newest entries appear at the **bottom** — navigate forward (→) through time, backward (←) for older.

For the curated talk version, see [chapter4-slides.html](chapter1-slides.html).

## Status legend

- ✅ figure finalized for chapter
- 🔄 figure under iteration
- ⚠ result preliminary, do not cite

. . .

The "What we did" lines are pulled from the daily-summaries the nightly loop writes; figures are mirrored from `chapter-4-biogeochem-geofish/figures/`.

# Timeline {.center}

_New entries auto-appended below this divider every night._

<!-- PROGRESS-INSERT-MARKER — phase 5 of the nightly pipeline appends new sections HERE -->

---

## 2026-04-23 — Nightly pipeline {.smaller}

**Literature:** Phase-4 AUTO block appended to `chapter4.qmd` and `literature-review.md` on Eastern Tropical Pacific OMZ–fish dynamics.

- Clarke et al. (2022): ocean warming primary driver of large pelagic catch composition changes in Ecuador/Galápagos EEZs; OMZ amplifies vulnerability during extreme El Niño events.
- Taboada et al. (2022): 3-D biogeochemical forecasts outperform surface-only predictors for bigeye tuna CPUE; four biogeochemical regimes 1960–2016.
- Galloso et al. (2025): six decades of Humboldt Current pelagic community data — DO + temperature explain compositional shifts; warmer/less-oxygenated conditions drive higher diversity via habitat compression.

**Bib:** 3 new entries (`clarke2022pelagic`, `taboada2022bigeye`, `galloso2025humboldt`). All 10 citations resolve ✓.

## References {.scrollable}

::: {#refs}
:::
